title: decide fails on an injectivity goal over an infinite domain
informal_statement: A goal `Function.Injective (fun n : ℕ => n + 5)` attacked by `decide` instead of unfolding injectivity by hand.

failure.input_context_summary: Goal was `Function.Injective (fun n : ℕ => n + 5)`, an elementary injectivity fact over the infinite type ℕ.
failure.attempt_summary: Applied `decide` directly to the injectivity goal, expecting it to close the way `decide` closes small finite-domain propositions. Lean failed to elaborate the tactic: 'failed to synthesize Decidable (Function.Injective fun n => n + 5)'.
failure.lesson: `decide` needs a global `Decidable` instance for the whole proposition, not just its atoms. A `∀`-statement over an infinite domain (`Function.Injective f` on ℕ or ℤ) has no such instance and will never get one; unfold it by hand (`intro a b h`) and close it with a real tactic (e.g. `omega`, `simpa`) instead of reaching for `decide`.

notes: Tracked via proofsearch episode 2874844f-84d1-476e-92b9-5cfe043a88cf: step 1 attempted `decide` and hit a kernel_fail diagnostic ('failed to synthesize Decidable (Function.Injective fun n => n + 5)'); step 2 gave_up, closing the episode honestly rather than pasting in a side-channel fix. Illustrative v0.1 negative example. Labeled non-proof-bearing training data (rung 0).
